event.evidence:
Golden invariant #2 holds. Negation is computed in Rust after exit-code classification, never via a shell. run_check dispatches cmd checks to run_cmd (check.rs:325), which executes the raw command as `sh -c <run>` (check.rs:1041-1042, never wrapped in `!`), classifies via classify_exit, then applies apply_negation (check.rs:419-420). classify_exit (check.rs:942-952) is a total match with no wildcard: only exit 0 -> Held, exit 1 -> Drifted; every other exit code, Signalled, TimedOut, SpawnFailed, and NotExecuted -> Broken. apply_negation (check.rs:961-970) swaps Held<->Drifted only and passes Broken|Unverifiable through unchanged, so a missing binary (exit 127), non-0/1 exit, signal death, timeout, or spawn failure stays Broken under negate. An empty `run` returns Broken before negation is applied (check.rs:404-413). Agent checks have no negate field and negation is not applied to them. verdict.rs:34-38 documents the same contract, and tests at check.rs:1732-1778 assert every Broken-stays-Broken-under-negate path.
citations:
- crates/claim-core/src/check.rs:325
- crates/claim-core/src/check.rs:404-427 (run_cmd)
- crates/claim-core/src/check.rs:942-952 (classify_exit)
- crates/claim-core/src/check.rs:961-970 (apply_negation)
- crates/claim-core/src/check.rs:1040-1046 (execute: sh -c <run>)
- crates/claim-core/src/check.rs:1732-1778 (negation tests)
- crates/claim-core/src/verdict.rs:19-38
